text.title: Partnership Co-Marketing Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Partnership Co-Marketing Playbook application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Target partners with 30-70% ICP overlap. <30% = audience won't convert; >70% = competitors.
rule.rationale: Industry data: partnership conversion peaks in the 30-70% overlap range; outside = waste.

rule.statement: Decide asset format upfront: webinar, research report, bundle, podcast. Joint creative direction is the #1 partner-friction source.
rule.rationale: Format-by-committee delays asset 6-12 weeks; pre-decided format ships in 4-6 weeks.

rule.statement: Joint UTM + dedicated landing + post-event survey. 'Who brought this lead?' must be answerable.
rule.rationale: Without attribution, both parties feel they contributed more; partnership erodes.

rule.statement: Balance: each party contributes roughly equal audience reach × asset effort. Asymmetric partnerships die in 1-2 cycles.
rule.rationale: Lopsided contribution leads to resentment; up-front balance check prevents it.

rule.statement: Quarterly: review attribution + leads + revenue with partner. Re-up or sunset based on data, not friendship.
rule.rationale: Without review, dead partnerships drag on; quarterly cadence forces honest evaluation.
